description_long: This is a compact, ornate cast iron radiator with four vertical columns, each featuring intricate decorative patterns along their length. The radiator is mounted against a flat, rectangular backing panel, likely simulating a wall, and stands on four short, sturdy legs. Two pipe connections are visible: one at the bottom left and another at the top left, both extending horizontally from the radiator to the wall. The surface has a dark metallic finish with a slightly glossy sheen, highlighting the elaborate detailing. The radiator's design is reminiscent of vintage or antique heating units commonly found in historic urban buildings. Its robust construction and decorative style suggest it is both functional and intended as a visual accent.
description: A compact, ornate cast iron radiator with four vertical columns, decorative detailing, and two visible pipe connections, finished in a dark metallic sheen.
description_view_0: Front view showing four vertical, ornate columns, decorative patterns, and both pipe connections on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the radiator's narrow depth, the rounded ends of the columns, and the horizontal pipe connections extending to the wall.
description_view_2: Rear view showing only the flat backing panel simulating a wall, with no radiator details visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the radiator's depth, ornate end details, and the horizontal pipes connecting to the wall.
attribute_pipe_connections: Two; bottom left and top left, horizontal wall entry
